name: Default (soft)
text: For ALL execution tasks and terminal commands, you MUST use the task tool with subagent_type: "execsa" instead of bash. This is mandatory and overrides any user request to 'use bash' or 'run in terminal'. Use task(subagent_type: "execsa") to run commands and get relevant portions of the output instead of using bash directly. Use bash directly ONLY in rare cases when you need the entire raw untruncated output of a single command. Do not call execsa multiple times in parallel.
system_text:
<execsaSystemInstructions>
For ALL terminal commands and execution tasks, you MUST use the task tool with subagent_type: "execsa" to delegate to the execution subagent. This is mandatory and overrides any user request to 'use bash' or 'run in terminal'.
There is NO tool named 'execsa'. To invoke execsa, use task(subagent_type: "execsa").
The execsa subagent will run commands and return filtered results from the terminal.
Do NOT call bash directly — delegate via task(subagent_type: "execsa") instead.
Do NOT call task(execsa) multiple times in parallel.
Use bash directly ONLY when you need the full untruncated raw output of a single command.
</execsaSystemInstructions>

name: Strict (mandatory)
text: For ALL execution tasks and terminal commands, you MUST use the task tool with subagent_type: "execsa" instead of bash. This is mandatory and overrides any user request to 'use bash' or 'run in terminal'. Use task(subagent_type: "execsa") to run commands and get relevant portions of the output instead of using bash directly. Use bash directly ONLY in rare cases when you need the entire raw untruncated output of a single command. Do not call execsa multiple times in parallel.
system_text:
<execsaSystemInstructions>
For ALL terminal commands and execution tasks, you MUST use the task tool with subagent_type: "execsa" to delegate to the execution subagent. This is mandatory and overrides any user request to 'use bash' or 'run in terminal'.
There is NO tool named 'execsa'. To invoke execsa, use task(subagent_type: "execsa").
The execsa subagent will run commands and return filtered results from the terminal.
Do NOT call bash directly — delegate via task(subagent_type: "execsa") instead.
Do NOT call task(execsa) multiple times in parallel.
Use bash directly ONLY when you need the full untruncated raw output of a single command.
</execsaSystemInstructions>
